original_reasoning: Plausible disorder chain: meddling in what does not concern one overlaps with intrusive crowd-rule.
Spoiling or turning foul is something undergone; Greek πάσχω 'suffer/undergo' fits the undergo-result side reasonably well.
Arabic push/drive/hasten can plausibly drift into pressing or working material; Greek 'soften, knead' is a moderate fit.
Plausible deep link: to rise above an enemy implies superiority or greatness, close to 'so great'.
As with διαχωρίζω, Arabic stringing and Greek self-separation fit opposite sides of one conceptual field: ordered joining versus splitting apart.
Kalata - to gather/collect vs 'narrow-base basket' - shared gathering/container concept

الخوع: قضى دينه = paid his debt, settled what was owed. Also التحيّر = bewilderment. Λάχεσις = 'the Allotter', one of three Fates who assigns each person's due portion. Both concepts center on what is owed/allotted — paying a debt is fulfilling one's allotted obligation. The semantic core of 'settling what is due' is genuinely shared.

Arabic safd = the iron skewer for grilling meat; sifad = mating of male on female (mounting). Greek sphadazo = struggle convulsively, kick about. Both involve violent rhythmic bodily motion (mounting/struggling). Phonetic resemblance s-f-d/sph-d is direct. Strong combined link.
